# Copilot instructions for Tactish

## Commands

Use npm; this repository has `package-lock.json`.

```bash
npm install
npm run dev      # Vite dev server
npm run build    # TypeScript type-check plus production Vite build
npm run preview  # Preview the production build
```

There are no configured test or lint scripts in `package.json`. If tests are added later, document both the full-suite command and the single-test command here.

## Architecture

Tactish is a client-only React 19 + TypeScript + Vite app styled with Tailwind CSS v4 through `@tailwindcss/vite` and `src\index.css`. `src\main.tsx` mounts `App`, and `src\App.tsx` owns the top-level `BrowserRouter`, navigation, and routes for the Game Assistant, Comp Manager, Comp Editor, and Database pages.

Set data is bundled as JSON under `src\data\set17\`. `src\hooks\useSetData.ts` is the central access point: it imports the JSON files, casts them to the shared types, and builds lookup maps such as `championById`, `itemById`, `traitById`, and `godById`. To add a future set, mirror the Set 17 folder structure and update `useSetData.ts`.

State is split across small Zustand stores:

- `src\stores\compStore.ts` persists user-created `TeamComp` data to localStorage key `tft-comps` and seeds sample comps from `App` on first load.
- `src\stores\settingsStore.ts` persists scoring weights and `activeSetId` to `tft-settings`; its custom merge keeps newly added default weights available after older settings are loaded.
- `src\stores\gameStore.ts` keeps the current in-game selections in memory only: champions, base components, and up to two gods.

The suggestion flow runs through `src\pages\GameAssistant.tsx`: it combines game selections, saved comps, set data, and settings, then calls `scoreAllComps` from `src\utils\scoring.ts`. Scoring combines rank baseline, direct champion matches, trait overlap, item component matches from `src\utils\items.ts`, and preferred-god matches. `CompCard`, `HexGrid`, and `ItemHelper` render the scored result details.

Comp management is centered on `TeamComp` from `src\types\index.ts`. `CompManager` filters/imports/exports persisted comps, while `CompEditor` owns the drag-and-drop board editor, item assignment, carry flags, preferred gods, and validation before saving. `Database` is derived from static set data plus saved comps to show champion, item, and trait relationships.

Static icon paths are generated by `src\utils\icons.ts` and served from `public\icons\...`; JSON ids, comp ids, and icon filenames must stay aligned.

## Conventions

- Use the shared domain types in `src\types\index.ts` for champions, items, comps, scoring, and store state. Keep JSON data ids as stable kebab-case strings because they are used in set data, saved comps, scoring, and public icon paths.
- Board positions are `{ row, col }` with rows `0-3` and columns `0-6`; board maps use the string key format `${row}-${col}`. Keep this convention when touching `HexGrid`, `CompEditor`, or suggestion detail rendering.
- Dummy units use ids beginning with `__dummy_` from `src\utils\dummy.ts`. They are placeholders for board layout and should be excluded from scoring, real champion counts, item matching, and trait counts unless a feature explicitly handles placeholders.
- Completed items have exactly two base component ids. Preserve multiplicity for component selections and recipes, including same-component recipes such as two swords.
- `TeamComp.champions[].items` is capped at three item ids, and `gameStore.selectedGodIds` is capped at the two most recent selections.
- Prefer the existing lookup maps from `useSetData()` over rebuilding maps in pages unless the page needs a page-specific aggregate.
- Styling is primarily Tailwind utility classes with occasional inline styles for board geometry, icon sizing, and drag/drop visuals. Keep the dark gray/yellow visual language consistent with existing pages.
